title: Badge
description: A badge communicates status, category, or count in a compact visual form.
whenToUse: To label status or state (active/pending/error). To communicate a category tag. To show counts or notifications.
whenNotToUse: For interactive filter tags, use a Chip component instead.
acceptanceCriteria: All five tones render in both prominent and subtle variants
Default and sm sizes render at the documented padding/typography
Text contrast against background meets WCAG AA in both light and dark modes for every variant
Badge does not receive focus and has no hover/active/disabled states
Color is never the sole indicator of meaning — every variant has a textual label

props.name: data-prominent
props.description: true = filled (high emphasis); false = subtle (tinted background, colored text)

props.name: data-size
props.description: Compact size for use inside dense surfaces (e.g. table cells, list rows)

slots.name: default
slots.description: The badge text content. Keep short — typically a status word, count, or category label.

states.name: default
states.description: Static visual indicator; no interactive states

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: Badge is not focusable or interactive — no hover, focus, active, or disabled states

contentGuidelines: Single short word or count, ideally ≤ 12 characters. Use sentence case for status labels ("Pending", not "PENDING"). Numeric badges should display "99+" rather than 4-digit counts.

dos: Convey meaning with text, not color alone — pair status colors with explicit words
Use the subtle variant when badges sit on a colored or busy surface
Keep tone consistent: success = positive, warning = needs attention, error = blocking
donts: Don't make a badge interactive — use a Chip for filter/dismiss/select behavior
Don't stack badges to communicate multiple statuses; pick the most important one
Don't use the error tone for non-blocking states like "draft"

keyboard.action: Not focusable; badges are decorative and convey meaning via their text content

screenReader.announcement: The badge text is read inline as part of the surrounding content; no role announcement is added

wcag.howMet: Tone is communicated by both color and the textual label, not color alone
Prominent and subtle variants both meet the 4.5:1 contrast ratio against their surface tokens

_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Badges are decorative by default; convey meaning via surrounding text as well. Do not rely on color alone — the label text carries the meaning.
